avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is low. In a few places, snowdrift accumulations can be triggered as small slab avalanches, especially in extremely steep and shady gullies and bowls. Avalanche prone locations and icy areas are covered with a thin layer of fresh snow. The risk of falling and injury is greater than the danger of burial.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Low avalanche danger, but watch out for older wind slabs in the north and east aspects!

snowpackStructure.comment: There is still significantly less snow than average. From midday, a few centimetres of new fallen snow may cover small, older snowdrift accumulations. Where there is a snow base, fundament (shady areas at high altitudes), angular snow crystals will weaken the stability of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights:
Wednesday will bring lots of clouds, it will become milder and it will slowly start to rain or snow at the highest altitudes. The precipitation intensifies towards the evening and the snowfall level slowly drops.
The avalanche risk will remain low on Wednesday, but may increase afterwards depending on the amount of new snow.

weatherForecast.comment: Tuesday night will bring starry conditions on the Lower Austrian mountains. In the morning hours, dense clouds will gather and in the afternoon it may snow lightly. The wind will be strong to stormy from the south-west. The temperature will be between 0 and +5 degrees at an altitude of 1500 metres.

avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is low. Occasionally, small slab avalanches can be triggered, especially in extremely steep and shady gullies and bowls. Avalanche prone locations as well as icy areas are often covered by some new fallen snow. Beware of persistent danger of falling!
avalancheActivity.highlights: Low avalanche danger - only isolated avalanche prone locations

snowpackStructure.comment: There is still significantly less snow than average. A thin layer of fresh snow is covering the hard old snowpack that still exists in some places, or areas that are still bare. Edged snow crystals can weaken the snow base, fundament.

tendency.highlights:
Wednesday will bring lots of clouds, it will become milder and it will slowly start to rain or snow at the highest altitudes. The precipitation intensifies towards the evening and the snowfall level slowly drops.
The avalanche risk will remain low on Wednesday, but may increase afterwards depending on the amount of new snow.

weatherForecast.comment: Tuesday night will bring starry conditions on the Lower Austrian mountains. In the morning hours, dense clouds will gather and in the afternoon it may snow lightly. The wind will be strong to stormy from the south-west. The temperature will be between 0 and +5 degrees at an altitude of 1500 metres.

ALBINA.generalHeadline: Low avalanche danger, but still isolated avalanche prone locations!
